name: Moqueca De Peixe e Camarao (Bahian Fish Stew)
recipeIngredient: 6 thick fish steaks; seabass, pompano or grouper
1 lb small shrimp, cleaned and deveined
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the fish with lemon and salt. Set aside.
2. Thinly slice onions, green bell pepper, and tomatoes.
3. Cook onions in olive oil until translucent.
4. Add green bell pepper and cook for 3 minutes.
5. Add tomatoes to the pan and cook for a few more minutes.
6. Place fish in the pan, top with Dende oil, cover the pan, and cook until almost cooked through.
7. Add chopped herbs, red pepper, and salt to taste. Cook uncovered over high heat to evaporate some of the liquid.
8. Just before serving, add coconut milk and bring nearly to a boil.
This is a dish from northeast Brazil. Dende, a thick dark orange oil, is an essential ingredient in Bahian cooking. It can be found in ethnic South American stores.
